Title: Import unification (grid-detect any dataset; geometry section conditional)
Notes:
Merges model-preflight with aux-import; biggest new surface. SCOPED 2026-06-17 (Explore-mapped the two import surfaces: model=preflight.js/#panelPreflight w/ XYZ+DXYZ+geometry; comparisons=auxtab.js/#panelAux clones, XYZ-only, geometry discarded). DECISION (Arthur): grid classification = AUTO-detect + VISIBLE + OVERRIDE (no-magic-only-ui). Slices: 4f-1 foundation ¬∑ 4f-2 import UI (conditional geometry/DXYZ section + grid badge/override in dataset panels) ¬∑ 4f-3 display (generalize Summary Grid-Geometry table beyond model-only lastGeoData) ¬∑ 4f-4 de-special-case naming/branches. **4f-1 DONE: worker computeGeometry emits per-axis `regularity` (dominant-spacing share of gaps) + geometry-level `isRegularGrid` (all 3 axes blockSize + regularity>=0.5) ‚Äî ADDITIVE (b1-differential: all 29 cases byte-identical w/ --ignore-keys=isRegularGrid,regularity; existing geometry/stats unchanged). Comparison-dataset complete handler now captures m.geometry (was discarded). dsGridMode(ds) tri-state grid/point/auto (default model='grid' [explicit block-model import], else 'auto'); dsGrid gates: blockSize-on-all-axes AND (mode grid | (auto & isRegularGrid)); point forces null. Model bit-identical (grandfathered via gridMode default). a10-smoke: model still grid, d2(model centroids) auto-detects grid, gridMode='point' override forces non-grid, aux(samples) not a grid. c6/gt-theo/topcut green. ds.gridMode override is in-memory in 4f-1; UI + persistence land in 4f-2. **4f-2 DONE (2026-06-17): Block geometry section in BOTH import surfaces (model preflight #pfGridWrap + comparison panels [data-aux=gridWrap]). Grid/point/auto override = segmented .ds-grid-chip control (model default grid, comparisons auto) + post-analysis detected badge (‚ñ¶ grid <sizes> / ‚à¥ points) w/ disagreement note ‚Äî no-magic-only-ui. Shared dsGridSectionHtml(ds)+dsHasXYZ(ds) in core.js; badge refresh via refreshModelGridSection() (displayResults) + refreshAuxGridSection(ds,root) (aux complete) since neither sidebar rebuilds post-analysis. gridMode now a real registry field: model/aux views back currentGridMode/auxGridMode, dsCreate carries it for d2+. Title: Per-dataset filter from context menu + filtered-size preview
Notes: Arthur 2026-06-16. Tree-ds datasetProvider gets Filter‚Ä¶ (‚úì when set) ‚Üí #dsFilterModal editing that dataset's .filter (createExprInput, filter mode). Worker 'count' mode (countAnalysis: makeRowSource + src.stream, one pass, counts total + filter survivors, surfaces filterErrors/calcolErrors) ‚Üí count-complete {total,kept}. Apply sets ds.filter + re-analyzes (model=startAnalysis+currentFilter sync, cmp=runAuxAnalysis which reads ds.filter; syncs an open config-panel filter textarea). Dataset-generic. INSTANT estimate (Arthur's steer ‚Äî model from the t-digests, NOT the file head): dsFilterSynthEstimate Monte-Carlos 4000 synthetic rows from each column's t-digest centroids + category counts (cumulative-weight arrays, binary-searched; reuses generateSyntheticRows sampling), runs the real compiled filter (+calcols). Sort-order UNBIASED + accurate for single-column predicates (smoke: Fe>50 ‚Üí modeled 55% vs exact 53%, vs the biased file HEAD's 0%). CAVEAT surfaced honestly ('modeled ‚Äî assumes columns vary independently, so multi-column filters may differ'): marginals lose JOINT correlation, so multi-column filters on correlated cols can be off. Head-sniff kept only as the not-yet-analyzed fallback (labeled non-representative). Reliable size = the exact one-pass count. Smoke experiments/filter-modal-smoke.js (modeled‚âàexact, exact proven against post-Apply rowCount). b1-differential 29/29 bit-identical. NEXT polish if wanted: auto-exact for small files.

Title: Copula-modeled filter estimate (OPTIONAL ‚Äî multi-column accuracy)
Notes: Optional follow-on to A14 ‚Äî improves the INSTANT filtered-size estimate for MULTI-COLUMN filters on correlated columns (today dsFilterSynthEstimate samples each column's marginal independently, so joint correlation is lost). Discussed Arthur+Claude 2026-06-16. Literal multivariate t-digest = NOT a thing (no canonical total order / quantile in >=2D). Realistic path = GAUSSIAN COPULA: keep the existing per-column t-digest marginals + accumulate ONE d√ód correlation matrix in the SAME analysis streaming pass (Welford sum-of-products, no extra I/O); estimate-time = Cholesky ‚Üí draw correlated normals ‚Üí push through each column's t-digest inverse-CDF ‚Üí run the real filter. COST: memory O(d¬≤) trivial (d=30‚Üí~3.7KB vs digests' 10s-100s KB); CPU O(d¬≤)/row added to the analysis hot path ‚Äî free at d‚â§~20, ~seconds at d=100 over 1M rows (CAP via top-K cols or a d-threshold). CAPTURES monotone/linear dependence (good for grades); MISSES nonlinear/tail dependence; categoricals don't fit Pearson (one-hot blows up d ‚Üí keep them marginal). RECOMMENDATION (Claude): do NOT bake into the default analyze pass ‚Äî it taxes a hot path for ALL users to improve ONE preview on a SUBSET of filters, and the exact one-pass count is already truth-on-tap. Build only as OPT-IN / lazy (compute the matrix only when enabled or d below a cap), or skip entirely and rely on Exact count. Single-column predicates already accurate w/o it. Needs: corr matrix in worker complete msg + analysisFingerprint + serialize/cache plumbing.

SIMULATION-VALIDATION surface: check a set of geostatistical SIMULATIONS reproduces its inputs ‚Äî histogram/CDF reproduction, variogram reproduction, category proportions, ergodic fluctuations across realizations, swath/Q‚ÄìQ vs the data. CONCRETE viz (friend cited RMSP): a CATEGORICAL proportion-reproduction plot ‚Äî each realization's per-category proportion plotted against the DATA proportion as a reference line, so you see at a glance whether the sims honor the global proportions and how much they spread (ergodic fluctuation). Reuses the A19 categorical machinery across realizations-as-datasets. KEY FIT: the N-DATASET workspace handles this naturally ‚Äî realizations ARE datasets, so reproduction-checks reuse the existing Stats/CDF/Swath/Categories/Œî% machinery across realizations-as-datasets. Title: Export presets + bound output (one-click re-export)
Notes: Arthur 2026-06-21 ‚Äî a mode to set an OUTPUT CSV for a given export + a quick context-menu 're-export' button, so the CSV stays in sync for whatever downstream software re-reads it (Vulcan/Leapfrog/Datamine/Isatis). Turns BMA into a PIPELINE COMPONENT: tweak the model/filter/calcols ‚Üí one click ‚Üí the bound CSV refreshes ‚Üí the other tool picks it up. TWO LAYERS: (1) EXPORT PRESETS ‚Äî a named, saved export config (column selection + format + filter + target dataset, all of which G5 already made per-dataset) re-runnable with one click ‚Üí re-download (works TODAY, no FSAA; persists in the project as an export-presets list; re-export affordance = tree/dataset context-menu 'Re-export to <name>' + an Export-tab button; buildable-now contained slice). (2) BOUND OUTPUT FILE ‚Äî the preset additionally remembers an FSAA showSaveFilePicker handle so re-export OVERWRITES that file IN PLACE (no download dialog) ‚Äî the true 'keep this CSV in sync' bridge; handle persists in IDB (the C11 machinery); pairs with C11/FSAA folders. Connects: builds on G5 (per-dataset/cloneable Export) + C11 (FSAA handles, write-to-file) + the A11 materialize concept (write derived to a file, recipe link kept ‚Äî an export preset IS a lightweight materialize-on-demand). SAFETY: overwriting a bound file = the no-silent-loss rule ‚Üí the binding is visible + confirmed before first write (no-magic-only-ui). Sequencing: presets layer ships pre-FSAA; bound-handle layer pairs with C11. Design when picked up (roadmap row for now).

Title: Composite length-distribution histograms (compositing QA)
Notes: DONE (2026-06-21, viz-only, no engine change). Twin before/after support-length histogram in the drillhole composite report: BEFORE = raw imported interval lengths (TO‚àíFROM of D.parsed.intervals); AFTER = composite SUPPORT (covered length, result col 6). dhComputeLengthHist(D,rows) (captured in dhCompositeAndLoad) + dhLengthHistHtml (shared bin edges over [0,max], 14 bins, twin bar-rows w/ n+mean labels, before=--fg-dim / after=--action) appended to renderDhReport; reset in dhClearAll. The geologist sees the support regularize (varied ‚Üí ~L) + the short/low-coverage tail the min-coverage filter targets. CSS .dh-hist*. Smoke dh-histogram-smoke.js (9: before n=4 mean 1.25 varied ‚Üí after n=5 mean 1.00 regular, shared bins, distinct classes, clears w/ the set). drillhole/dh-merge/all compositing+pack+a9/c6/oracle green. Arthur 2026-06-21 (off the A11 compositing-surface work). The compositing surface should show length-distribution histograms BEFORE and AFTER compositing ‚Äî the raw interval lengths (TO‚àíFROM of the source interval table) vs the composite SUPPORT (covered length) of the produced composites ‚Äî side by side, so the geologist sees at a glance how compositing regularized the support (the classic "did my 2 m composites actually come out ~2 m, and what's the short-tail/low-coverage population" QA). Data is already on hand: source lengths from the interval table parsed rows (TO‚àíFROM); composite lengths = the SUPPORT column the engine already emits (covered length, A7 D5). Likely a small twin-histogram panel in the drillhole card (post-composite) or a section in the report; ties to min-coverage (the short-support population the filter targets is visible). Pairs with the existing consistency report. Design when picked up; small, viz-only (no engine change ‚Äî SUPPORT already exists). Could generalize to any length/weight column histogram.

Title: Dataset join ‚Äî key + spatial (migration / reblocking)
Notes: Arthur 2026-06-21 (off the A19 cross-dataset nuance + 2nd-round friend feedback) ‚Äî 'we will need a good way to JOIN datasets'. The ROW-LEVEL-PAIRING keystone: where C10 made datasets talk at the DISTRIBUTION level, the join makes them talk at the ROW level. TWO modes: (1) KEY JOIN (relational, SQL/pandas merge) ‚Äî join on a shared key column (BHID, block id, a categorical); inner/left/right/outer; derived dataset carries both sides' columns on matched rows. GENERALIZES the A11 down-hole interval merge (already a specialized BHID+FROM/TO RANGE join). (2) SPATIAL JOIN (no shared key, match by XYZ) ‚Äî (a) NEAREST/CONTAINING MIGRATION: for each target row find the source at/near its location, copy values (assign sample‚Üíblock, model-A class‚Üímodel-B blocks; 'neo's migration', a ../neo sibling-lib adapter); (b) REBLOCK/AGGREGATE: aggregate source rows within each target block (mean grade, sum tonnes, majority domain, length/mass-weighted) = 'copy statistics' for reblocking a fine SMU model to mining blocks. BOTH produce a DERIVED dataset ‚Üí slots into C12 lineage (joined‚ÜêA+B, re-derivable + materializable). UNLOCKS: A19 cross-dataset crosstab/confusion-matrix, A12 contact analysis, reconciliation, row-level model-vs-samples (paired Q‚ÄìQ/Œî%), reblocking, A20 sim-vs-data pairing. As foundational as C10. Vendor ../neo (spatial) when picked up. Design doc when picked up ‚Äî the big architectural enabler for the comparison/reconciliation family.

Title: Derivation lineage (the derived-data DAG)
Notes: **P1 DONE (2026-06-22, commits 235d501 P1a + 2c54801 P1b): staleness propagation over the DAG + unified Refresh surface. P1a: derivation.js derivConsumers/derivMarkStale/derivSetNodeStale/derivRefresh ‚Äî a change at one node flags every node DOWNSTREAM stale (topological, visited-guarded); single-source (analysis node stale = ds.stale/analysisStale, composite = new D._stale); derivSetNodeStale routes through the existing markers (markAuxStale/markAnalysisStale) so no second copy of truth. Composite goes stale on any recipe/source change after one was produced (re-drop / opt / mapping / split / density / combine / convention edit) ‚Äî dhReflectStale shows the '‚Üª recipe changed ‚Äî re-composite' cue via the C6-5 gen-stale pattern (no gen-done dimming ‚Üí fresh look unchanged), dhMarkCompositeStaleIfLoaded propagates to the analysis; cleared on re-composite. P1b: catalog-tree dataset nodes get a 'stale' badge + ‚Üª Refresh (treeDsRefreshHtml in tree.js, injected in the summary; click routes [data-refresh] through derivRefresh w/ preventDefault; composite-target preferred so re-composite cascades, else re-analyze); generalizes executeBtn+Analyze buttons into ONE surface; markAnalysisStale/markAuxStale now renderTree so the badge tracks edits outside the propagation path; CSS .tree-ds-stale/.tree-ds-refresh (warn). Guard c12-derivation-smoke.js 25 asserts; drillhole/a10/tree/rails/delta-row/a9/c6 green.** 1.4 PLAN (2026-06-22, docs/1.4-plan.md Track 1 ‚Äî the platform keystone): P0 name the Derivation contract + retrofit drillhole composite & aux INERT/bit-identical (C10-P0 move) -> P1 staleness propagation + ONE stale-badge/Refresh surface (subsumes dhStale/markAuxStale/re-analyze). P2 (emit/materialize/re-export AS Derivations) + P3 (air IR + pushdown w/ B2) HELD to 1.5. **P0 DONE (2026-06-22): src/derivation.js ‚Äî makeDerivation contract {id,kind,sources,derive,stale,mode,output} + READ-ONLY projection derivAll()/derivById() expressing the two live recompute mechanisms (each dataset's analysis + each drillhole composite) AS Derivations over live state. INERT: nothing drives behavior from it; stale getters MIRROR the existing flags (model analysisStale, others ds.stale); DAG edge collar+survey+intervals->composite->analysis (analysis:aux sources composite:aux when dh-derived). Added to APP_MODULES after core.js. Guard experiments/c12-derivation-smoke.js (16 asserts incl bit-identical 20-composite canary). drillhole-smoke + a10-smoke green (bit-identical).** Arthur 2026-06-21 ‚Äî the unifying interface under A11 (composite/merge/emit/materialize) + A18 (re-export) + calcols. EVIDENCE: 6 kinds of derived data (composite/merged/emitted/materialized/export-output/calcol-cols) each hand-roll their own staleness+recompute (model re-analyze, aux markAuxStale, drillhole dhStale, export none) ‚Äî the SAME missing-interface smell C10 just paid off for targeting. They form a DAG (assays+litho‚Üímerged‚Üícomposite‚Üíemitted‚Üíre-export; model‚Üícalcol‚Üífilter). Name a Derivation contract {id, sources[], derive(), stale, mode:linked|materialized, output?}: mode = the A11 decision generalized (linked=re-derive, materialized=freeze+keep-link, output‚ÜíC11 folder/A18 file); staleness PROPAGATES topologically (a source change marks downstream stale) + REFRESH on demand w/ a visible stale-badge (no silent stale ‚Äî A9 rule generalized to derived data). It's the DatasetSource pattern for the WRITE side (DatasetSource=how a ds is read; Derivation=how a ds came to be) + the data model for C7 provenance/report. PUSHDOWN BRIDGE (Arthur): route filters/calcols through an expression IR (../auditable/ext/air) so derive()s become ANALYZABLE ‚Äî static predicate extraction ‚Üí parquet/sidecar zone maps (principled vs io-architecture ¬ß2 regex shape-match), still compile to JS for the row path, ENABLE the B2 vectorized fast path (no-calcol+pushdownable-filter ‚Üí column-vectorized, skip row groups, reconstruct rows only for survivors). Phasing: P0 name+retrofit composite/aux inert ‚Üí P1 staleness propagation + one stale/Refresh surface (subsumes dhStale/markAuxStale) ‚Üí P2 emit/materialize/re-export build AS Derivations ‚Üí P3 air IR+pushdown w/ B2. Design: docs/derivation-lineage.md.

Open: reference-vs-copy source files (lean reference inputs / write derived); never overwrite a non-authored file (no-silent-loss extends to disk). Design of record: docs/fsaa-project-folders.md.
